section.heading: Program authorized

subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary may carry out a charter school program that supports charter schools that serve early childhood, elementary school, or secondary school students by—

paragraph.content: supporting the startup of new charter schools, the replication of high-quality charter schools, and the expansion of high-quality charter schools;
assisting charter schools in accessing credit to acquire and renovate facilities for school use; and

paragraph.chapeau: carrying out national activities to support—

subparagraph.content: the dissemination of best practices of charter schools for all schools;
the evaluation of the impact of the charter school program under this part on schools participating in such program; and

subsection.heading: Funding allotment
subsection.chapeau: From the amount made available under section 7221j of this title for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall—

paragraph.content: reserve 12.5 percent to support charter school facilities assistance under section 7221c of this title;
reserve 22.5 percent to carry out national activities under section 7221d of this title; and
use the remaining amount after the reservations under paragraphs (1) and (2) to carry out section 7221b of this title.

content.p: The recipient of a grant or subgrant under part B of title V (as such part was in effect on the day before December 10, 2015) shall continue to receive funds in accordance with the terms and conditions of such grant or subgrant.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 89–10, title IV, § 4302, as added Pub. L. 114–95, title IV, § 4301(1), Dec. 10, 2015, 129 Stat. 1994.)

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Part B of title V (as such part was in effect on the day before December 10, 2015), referred to in subsec. (c), means part B of title V of Pub. L. 89–10, which was classified generally to part B (§ 7221 et seq.) of subchapter V of this chapter prior to being amended by Pub. L. 114–95. Pub. L. 114–95, title IV, §§ 4001(b)(1)(B), (2), 4301, Dec. 10, 2015, 129 Stat. 1967, 1993, repealed subparts 2 (§ 7223 et seq.) and 3 (§ 7225 et seq.) of part B of subchapter V, redesignated the remainder of part B as this part, struck out the subpart 1 designation and heading, repealed and amended various sections of this part, and added several new sections. For complete classification of Pub. L. 114–95 to the Code, see Tables.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 7221a, Pub. L. 89–10, title IV, § 4302, formerly title V, § 5202, as added Pub. L. 107–110, title V, § 501, Jan. 8, 2002, 115 Stat. 1788; renumbered title IV, § 4302, Pub. L. 114–95, title IV, § 4001(b)(2)(A), (B), (D)(iii), Dec. 10, 2015, 129 Stat. 1967, related to authorization of grant program, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 114–95, § 5, title IV, § 4301(1), Dec. 10, 2015, 129 Stat. 1806, 1993, effective Dec. 10, 2015, except with respect to certain noncompetitive programs and competitive programs.
A prior section 4302 of Pub. L. 89–10 was renumbered section 8572, and is classified to section 7972 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Dec. 10, 2015, except with respect to certain noncompetitive programs and competitive programs, see section 5 of Pub. L. 114–95, set out as an Effective Date of 2015 Amendment note under section 6301 of this title.
